steps.title: Identify the doubling‑time pattern
steps.content:
The function is $$f(t)=8000\cdot 2^{t/300}$$.
For any exponential of the form $$A\cdot 2^{t/k}$$ the factor 2 appears when the exponent increases by 1, i.e., when $$t$$ increases by $$k$$.
Thus the population doubles after an additional $$k$$ minutes.

steps.title: Compute the doubling time
steps.content:
Here $$k=300$$, so the time required to double is $$300$$ minutes.
That matches choice B — B is correct.
